def: Concealment; seclusion.The mental organization of the novelist must be characterized, tospeak craniologically, by an extraordinary development of the passionfor delitescency. Sir W. Scott.

A larval entozoön in the form of a subglobular or oval vesicle,or hy datid, filled with fluid, sometimes found in the tissues of manand the lower animals; -- so called from the absence of a head orvisible organs on the vesicle. These cysts are the immature stages ofcertain tapeworms. Also applied to similar cysts of different origin.
An order of birds, including the swans, ducks, geese,flamingoes and screamers.
Of or pertaining to the tribe of ruminants of which the goat,or genus Capra, is the type.
One who dwells in the same house with another. R. Browning.
Any insect of the genus Perla, or family Perlidæ. See Stonefly, under Stone.
Seed of the Hibiscus abelmoschus, somewhat resembling millet,brought from Egypt and the West Indies, and having a flavor like thatof musk; musk seed. Chambers.
